name: FastRouter

dataResidency.euRegionDetails: FastRouter states that it shares prompts and related request data with third-party model providers to fulfill API requests, and its routing/fallback features allow requests to be routed across multiple providers. No primary-source documentation was found guaranteeing EU-only processing or EU-region pinning.

dataUsage.retentionPolicy: Prompt data is stored temporarily for routing purposes and is typically deleted after 7 days. Usage logs may be retained for up to 90 days for monitoring and debugging purposes. Account information is retained for as long as the account is active or as needed to provide services and comply with legal obligations.
dataUsage.details: FastRouter says it stores prompts temporarily to fulfill requests and route them to the appropriate model provider, and shares prompts and related request data with third-party model providers such as OpenAI and Anthropic to fulfill API requests.

notes: FastRouter publishes a privacy policy with concrete retention periods for prompt data (typically 7 days) and usage logs (up to 90 days), but no primary-source DPA, sub-processor list, SCC statement, headquarters country, or security certification evidence was found. Because FastRouter routes prompts to third-party model providers and supports automatic routing/fallback across providers, EU-only inference cannot be verified and data can leave the EU at inference.
